content.problemStatement:
Write a program that checks if a given number is divisible by both 3 and 5.

Check the sample input / output below for more clarity.


Output Format
If the number is divisible by both 3 and 5 - output 'Divisible by both 3 and 5'


If the number is not divisible by either 3 or 5 (or both of them) - output 'Not divisible by both 3 and 5'
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10^5), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (−10^9 ≤ N ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing either "Divisible by both 3 and 5" if N is divisible by both 3 and 5, otherwise output "Not divisible by both 3 and 5".

sampleTestCases.explanation: 15 is divisible by both 3 and 5, so the condition holds.
20 is divisible by 5 but not by 3, therefore the output is the negative case.
Zero is divisible by any non‑zero integer; both remainders are zero.

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.expectedOutput: Divisible by both 3 and 5\nDivisible by both 3 and 5\nNot divisible by both 3 and 5

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput: Divisible by both 3 and 5\nNot divisible by both 3 and 5

solution.approach: For each test case we simply check whether N % 3 == 0 and N % 5 == 0. The modulo operator works for negative numbers as well: a number is divisible by k if the remainder is exactly zero. This yields O(1) work per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
